what_it_measures: the amount of hemoglobin, an iron-rich protein in red blood cells that carries oxygen
low_meaning: low hemoglobin commonly suggests anemia
high_meaning: high hemoglobin may occur with dehydration
dietary_note: iron-rich foods with vitamin C may help

what_it_measures: the total number of white blood cells
low_meaning: may be associated with some infections or medications
high_meaning: may suggest infection or inflammation
dietary_note: a balanced diet supports immune health

what_it_measures: the number of platelets that help form clots
low_meaning: may increase bruising or bleeding risk
high_meaning: may occur with infection, inflammation, or iron deficiency
dietary_note: good overall nutrition is generally recommended

what_it_measures: the percentage of blood volume made up of red blood cells
low_meaning: may indicate anemia or blood loss
high_meaning: may occur with dehydration

what_it_measures: the average size of your red blood cells
low_meaning: smaller cells, often iron deficiency anemia
high_meaning: larger cells, may relate to B12/folate deficiency or liver/alcohol causes
dietary_note: iron, B12, or folate may matter depending on the cause

what_it_measures: the number of red blood cells, which carry oxygen, in a volume of blood
low_meaning: a low RBC count can be associated with anaemia or blood loss
high_meaning: a high RBC count can be associated with dehydration or conditions that increase red-cell production
dietary_note: iron, vitamin B12 and folate are commonly discussed for healthy red-cell production

what_it_measures: the average amount of haemoglobin in a single red blood cell
low_meaning: a low MCH can be associated with iron-deficiency anaemia
high_meaning: a high MCH can be associated with vitamin B12 or folate deficiency
dietary_note: iron, B12 and folate-rich foods are commonly discussed for red-cell health

what_it_measures: the average concentration of haemoglobin in a given volume of red blood cells
low_meaning: a low MCHC can be associated with iron-deficiency anaemia
high_meaning: a high MCHC is uncommon and can be seen in certain inherited red-cell conditions
dietary_note: iron-rich foods are commonly discussed for low MCHC

what_it_measures: the variation in size of red blood cells (red-cell distribution width)
low_meaning: a low RDW is generally not a concern
high_meaning: a high RDW can be associated with mixed or evolving anaemias, where red cells vary more in size
dietary_note: the dietary measures discussed for anaemia apply here

what_it_measures: the percentage of neutrophils, white cells that fight bacterial infection, among white blood cells
low_meaning: a low neutrophil percentage can be associated with some viral infections or bone-marrow effects
high_meaning: a high neutrophil percentage can be associated with bacterial infection, inflammation or physical stress

what_it_measures: the percentage of lymphocytes, white cells central to the immune response, among white blood cells
low_meaning: a low lymphocyte percentage can be associated with some infections or immune conditions
high_meaning: a high lymphocyte percentage can be associated with viral infections

what_it_measures: the percentage of monocytes, white cells that clear debris and support immunity, among white blood cells
low_meaning: a low monocyte percentage is usually not a concern
high_meaning: a high monocyte percentage can be associated with chronic infection or inflammation

what_it_measures: the percentage of eosinophils, white cells involved in allergy and parasite response, among white blood cells
low_meaning: a low eosinophil percentage is usually not a concern
high_meaning: a high eosinophil percentage can be associated with allergies, asthma or parasitic infection

what_it_measures: the percentage of basophils, white cells involved in allergic and inflammatory responses, among white blood cells
low_meaning: a low basophil percentage is usually not a concern
high_meaning: a high basophil percentage is uncommon and can accompany certain allergic or bone-marrow conditions

what_it_measures: the actual number of neutrophils in a volume of blood
low_meaning: a low absolute neutrophil count (neutropenia) can be associated with a higher risk of infection
high_meaning: a high absolute neutrophil count can be associated with infection, inflammation or stress

what_it_measures: the actual number of lymphocytes in a volume of blood
low_meaning: a low absolute lymphocyte count can be associated with some infections or immune conditions
high_meaning: a high absolute lymphocyte count can be associated with viral infections

what_it_measures: the actual number of monocytes in a volume of blood
low_meaning: a low absolute monocyte count is usually not a concern
high_meaning: a high absolute monocyte count can be associated with chronic infection or inflammation

what_it_measures: the actual number of eosinophils in a volume of blood
low_meaning: a low absolute eosinophil count is usually not a concern
high_meaning: a high absolute eosinophil count can be associated with allergies or parasitic infection

what_it_measures: the actual number of basophils in a volume of blood
low_meaning: a low absolute basophil count is usually not a concern
high_meaning: a high absolute basophil count is uncommon and can accompany certain conditions

what_it_measures: mean platelet volume — the average size of platelets, which help blood clot
low_meaning: a low MPV can accompany certain bone-marrow conditions
high_meaning: a high MPV can indicate younger, larger platelets and can accompany higher platelet turnover

what_it_measures: platelet distribution width — the variation in platelet size
low_meaning: a low PDW is generally not a concern
high_meaning: a high PDW indicates more variation in platelet size and can accompany platelet disorders

what_it_measures: erythrocyte sedimentation rate — how quickly red cells settle, a general marker of inflammation
low_meaning: a low ESR is generally not a concern
high_meaning: a high ESR can be associated with inflammation or infection but is non-specific
